19. How to Create Real Scarcity Urgency.mp4 Okay, so I get this question a lot from sales teams that we work with, which is, how do you actually get people to take an action fast? Okay, and it all comes down to one thing. Now, one thing is very simple. Incentives. Okay. People take action based on having a reason to take that action. So a lot of organizations that we work with, they don't do discounts. They don't do... You know, any like time-based sales, you know, like they don't do Black Friday stuff. They don't do like themed holiday sales and anything like that. However. If we look at a lot of, let's use the example of the designer brand industry. So Louis Vuitton, Dior, Balenciaga, you know, even watch brands like Rolex or... or Richard Millay, Richard Milley, or, you know, any one of them, AP, all of these types of brands, they never do discounts. They have a recurring and evolving set of products that are limited, okay? So as an example, this very watch is a limited edition model. It came out, I think, sometime in the middle of 2012. It was manufactured between 2012 and 2015. It had a limited run of 250 pieces. And to be clear, after it was gone, it was gone, okay? Then... It's not like they never made another watch again. They just made other variations of watches. So let me give you a few examples. If I have a $40,000 automation business as an example, or if I'm trying to raise $100,000. Typically, especially for the investment niche, there's funds that only need a certain amount of revenue before they then close and they move on to another deal. So certain deals are more appealing than other deals to different types of investors. So let's say that I'm closing on some multifamily property in Texas somewhere in Dallas, let's say. Well, I'm only gonna acquire a certain property with that specific fund. And then once that fund closes, there is no other way to get into that deal. And then, from there, I'm gonna open another fund or I'm gonna open another syndicate or I'm just gonna focus on another property that we wanna acquire. So once again, the essential timeframe is, look, we're only raising a certain amount of capital. Once this amount of capital is raised, that syndicate specifically is going to close and then we're going to move on to other deals. If you want to get in, and you want to be in this specific deal, then you need to act right away. We have a handful of other investors that are putting up anywhere from $100K to over a million dollars, and once the round is closed, the round is closed. Know when that's going to happen. It could be literally tomorrow. It could be two, three weeks from now. But we want to make sure that we tell everybody who's considering to come in to get in sooner than later. And no, this is not made up. Once it's done, it's done. That's a good example of it. Or we can also look at the automation business where we have, let's say, a $40,000 Amazon automation store. Well, what's the reason that somebody's gonna- buy today if it's going to cost just as much next month or just as much the month after? So we don't really want to come up with discounts. We don't really want to come up with like random reasons for people to all of a sudden take action. We want to have... literally real things for like why people need to take action in a certain amount of time. So, as an example, maybe it's a tax incentive. So, hey, if you put the money up by this timeframe, you're going to be able to write that amount off for this year's tax bill. It could be other reasons like... Hey, Q4 is coming up. We have an unbelievable amount of work that we have to do in Q4, and there's a lot of sales that come in during that time frame. We have a 10% price bump that's going to start at the beginning of October. Just want to disclose that. Make sure you get in before then. Let's say that you're in the middle of Q4. Hey, there's an unbelievable volume of sales that are happening right now. We want to make sure that you can capitalize on it before the year ends. Let's get your store started today. Well, we can try to capitalize on some of these holiday sales coming in. Or let's say that it's the middle of the year. It's like April, you know, and somebody's considering buying that same $40,000 store. What is a real reason that somebody's going to buy it then versus the Q4 example I just gave? We have to be able to come up with reasons and ways that people are going to invest. Otherwise, there's no reason for them to. Incentives, okay? Let me give you an example. Fast action incentives. So hey, if you invest this $40,000 cost in your Amazon automation store by the end of the month, we're going to keep the cost at what it is. If you wait more than 30 days to do a deal with us from this conversation today, I just want to be clear. We have a 10% price bump. The cost will go up from $40,000 to $44,000. Because we like to incentivize action takers. We found that through the years, all the deals that we've done, the people who take action fast are the best clients to work with. And we want to reward that type of behavior. We want to attract that type of clientele. The people who are slow with their decision making can also slow down our ability to help you as a client in real time. If I need you to answer some simple question and you end up taking three weeks instead of just answering it the same day that I asked, that is going to drastically slow down the probability of us being able to get you results. So we punish the people who can't make decisions fast by adding price bumps. You know, just for whatever reason, to be clear, if you wait in that 30th to 60th day and you still don't take action, I want to be fair in letting you know that we have another 10% price bump and the price will go from 44K to about 48K and some change. Just to be fair. Like I said, if you take action between now and the next 30 days, which will give you plenty of time to review the deal with partners, review the deal with your spouse, review the deal yourself, review the deal with a lawyer, and really see. We'll even get you in contact with some of our clients if you'd like. So you can see why it's a great decision. And like I said, you'll get the best price in taking actions with us sooner than later. A simple incentive for a fast action taker can make or break whether somebody's going to do a deal or not. And it's not like you have to raise the price 10% for every other person you're going to sell to. You punish the people who are not action takers. And that's an example of a real reason. It gives real scarcity and it gives real urgency in your sales process to get people to move. So anyway, moral of the story, look at what you got going on in your deal, right? Think of, are there certain timeframes of the year where I could incentivize based on a tax deduction, or I can incentivize based on it's a heightened level of activity in comparison to every other year, or, Hey, it's the beginning of the year. Let's get started on the right foot. Let's attack your financial goals that you have. Hey, we're going to incentivize fast action takers. We'll give you the best price between now and the next 30 days. Otherwise, we punish you by doing this. And explain yourself. Explain why those incentives are there and why they exist. And make sure they're incentives that people actually give a shit about. That's the big difference. If somebody does not care about an incentive, they will not follow the incentive. Very simple.
